note: Public ontologies SMA can mount on demand (fetched lazily from source URLs, cached under SMA_CACHE_DIR). Science bulk = OBO Foundry registry; non-biomedical = curated+verified. 'mountable'=false entries are repo/API/page-only (listed for awareness; see homepage/notes to obtain). Some entries note license/registration caveats.

ontologies.name: Ontology for the Anatomy of the Insect SkeletoMuscular system (AISM)
The Amphioxus Development and Anatomy Ontology
Clytia hemisphaerica Development and Anatomy Ontology
The Echinoderm Anatomy and Development Ontology
The Ontology of Genes and Genomes
Drug-drug Interaction and Drug-drug Interaction Evidence Ontology
HUPO-PSI cross-linking and derivatization reagents controlled vocabulary
MITRE ATT&CK (Enterprise) — STIX 2.1 Knowledge Base
ontologies.license: Custom MITRE ATT&CK Terms of Use (free, attribution required; commercial use permitted)
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200, ~53 MB STIX 2.1 bundle of tactics/techniques/groups/software/mitigations. 'master' tracks the latest release; versioned files (e.g. enterprise-attack-15.1.json) also exist, plus mobile-attack and ics-attack domains in sibling folders. index.json lists all versions. Not an OWL ontology but the de-facto adversary-TTP vocabulary; widely RDF-ized downstream.

ontologies.name: CAPEC — Common Attack Pattern Enumeration and Classification
ontologies.license: Free public use under MITRE CAPEC Terms of Use (attribution requested)
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200, ~3.8 MB XML, 'capec_latest.xml' always points to the newest release (currently v3.9). CSV slices also available (e.g. https://capec.mitre.org/data/csv/2000.csv.zip). Stable direct download, no registration.

ontologies.name: CASE — Cyber-investigation Analysis Standard Expression
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200. Like UCO, master/case.ttl (~2 KB) is an owl:imports aggregator over ontology/<module>/ (investigation, vocabulary); clone repo or use releases for full content. CASE is the digital-forensics profile built on top of UCO; standardized via the Linux Foundation. Open license.

ontologies.name: CPE — Official Common Platform Enumeration Dictionary (NVD)
ontologies.license: U.S. Government public-domain data; free use (NVD Terms of Use)
ontologies.notes: HOMEPAGE + API. NIST retired the static gzipped XML CPE dictionary feed; current access is via the NVD CPE API 2.0 at https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cpes/2.0 (JSON, paginated, 10,000/page; an API key is recommended/free with registration). Data feed files reflecting the 2.0 /cpes/ and /cpematch/ schemas are linked from the NVD data-feeds page. No single stable bulk-XML direct download anymore — flag the API/pagination requirement.

ontologies.name: CVE List (v5) — Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures
ontologies.license: CVE Program Terms of Use (free to search, download, use and redistribute)
ontologies.notes: REPO + RELEASE-ZIPS, not one file. Official CVE List in 'CVE Record Format' (JSON 5.x), one JSON file per CVE under cves/<year>/. Bulk download via daily baseline zips on the Releases page (e.g. <date>_all_CVEs_at_midnight.zip) or git clone. Legacy CSV/XML/CVRF feeds were discontinued 30 Jun 2024. Free redistribution under CVE Program Terms of Use.

ontologies.name: CWE — Common Weakness Enumeration
ontologies.license: Free public use under MITRE CWE Terms of Use (attribution requested)
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200, ~2 MB zipped XML, 'cwec_latest.xml.zip' always resolves to the newest version (currently 4.x). Versioned archives at cwe.mitre.org/data/archive.html. Stable direct download, no registration.

ontologies.name: MITRE D3FEND — Defensive Countermeasure Ontology
ontologies.license: MITRE D3FEND Terms of Use; Approved for Public Release, Distribution Unlimited (NSA/MITRE funded)
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200, ~6.4 MB OWL 2 DL. Turtle also available at https://d3fend.mitre.org/ontologies/d3fend.ttl (~3.6 MB). Versioned IRIs exist, e.g. https://d3fend.mitre.org/ontologies/d3fend/1.4.0/d3fend.owl (verified 200). True formal cybersecurity ontology; current release 1.4.x. Government licensing terms apply but freely downloadable.

ontologies.name: MITRE Engage — Adversary Engagement / Denial & Deception Framework
ontologies.license: Custom MITRE Engage Terms of Use (free public use, attribution)
ontologies.notes: HOMEPAGE/REPO ONLY for the whole dataset — no single canonical ontology file. Format is plain JSON (not true JSON-LD/OWL). Raw data files live under Data/json/, e.g. https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mitre/engage/main/Data/json/attack_mapping.json (verified 200, ~282 KB) maps Engage activities to ATT&CK. Excel and other formats at the learn-more-data page. A framework taxonomy rather than a formal ontology.

ontologies.name: STIX 2.1 — Structured Threat Information Expression (OASIS) JSON Schemas
ontologies.license: BSD-3-Clause (schemas repo); STIX spec itself is OASIS open standard
ontologies.notes: REPO of canonical JSON Schemas for the STIX 2.1 object model (SDOs, SROs, cyber-observables). Format is JSON Schema, not OWL. The normative STIX 2.1 specification (PDF/HTML) is the OASIS standard; the schemas repo provides machine-readable validation. ATT&CK and many CTI tools serialize to this model. Open.

ontologies.name: STUCCO — Situation and Threat Understanding by Correlating Contextual Observations (ontology schema)
ontologies.notes: REPO ONLY. ORNL cyber knowledge-graph schema. NOTE: STUCCO's 'ontology' is expressed as JSON Schema / GraphSON (for graph-DB ingest), NOT OWL/RDFS — so it is a graph schema rather than a formal logical ontology. Integrates CVE/CPE/CWE/CAPEC/CVSS concepts. MIT-licensed, archived/research-stage (low recent activity).

ontologies.name: UCO — Unified Cyber Ontology
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200. NOTE: the master/uco.ttl is a small (~3 KB) owl:imports aggregator that pulls in all UCO modules (core, observable, action, identity, pattern, etc.) under ontology/uco/<module>/. Clone the repo or use a release tarball (latest tag 1.4.0 at https://github.com/ucoProject/UCO/releases) to get the full module set. Underpins CASE; OASIS-aligned. Open license.

ontologies.name: UCO (Ebiquity/UMBC) — Unified Cybersecurity Ontology (academic original)
ontologies.license: Open (academic release, no explicit SPDX license file in repo — treat as research/permissive)
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200, ~83 KB OWL/RDF. This is the SEPARATE, earlier UMBC Ebiquity 'Unified Cybersecurity Ontology' (Syed et al. 2016) — NOT the same as ucoProject/UCO above. Integrates STIX, CVE, CCE, CVSS, CAPEC, KillChain into one OWL model. Also ships uco2.ttl and stix2.ttl. No formal LICENSE file — flag for due diligence before redistribution.

ontologies.name: Food Interactions with Drugs Evidence Ontology
An ontology of core ecological entities
Environmental conditions, treatments and exposures ontology
Financial Industry Business Ontology (FIBO)
ontologies.notes: Flagship finance ontology from the EDM Council/OMG. Source of truth is the GitHub repo (RDF/XML .rdf files; OWL/Description Logic). Quarterly releases tagged on GitHub (latest master_2026Q1). Best obtained by cloning/downloading the repo tarball; per-version production zips and a 'fibo-quickstart' file are published under https://spec.edmcouncil.org/fibo/ontology/master/<YYYYQn>/ (e.g. prod.fibo-quickstart.ttl, prod.rdf.zip). License is MIT (LICENSE file in repo). Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: Financial Instrument Global Identifier (FIGI)
ontologies.notes: OMG open standard for unique financial-instrument identifiers (equities, bonds, derivatives, FX). Strictly an identifier scheme/spec + open API rather than a rich OWL ontology, so format is the OMG spec/UML+XMI and JSON API. Data access is via the OpenFIGI API/bulk files (free, registration for an API key recommended); the standard itself is MIT-licensed Open Data. Listed for completeness as a finance controlled-identifier resource; spec PDF at omg.org/figi/.

ontologies.name: Financial Regulation Ontology (FinRegOnt)
ontologies.notes: Open-source RDF/OWL ontology aligning FIBO (business) with LKIF (law) plus a full XBRL ontology; covers US Code/CFR law instances and SEC rules. Turtle instance/ontology files distributed from finregont.com (e.g. https://finregont.com/fro/usc/ instance dir). No single stable one-click bundle URL surfaced — homepage/reference-ontologies page given as entry point. Maintained by Jayzed Data Models. Free.

ontologies.name: GoodRelations (e-commerce vocabulary)
ontologies.notes: OWL 2 DL ontology for products, offers, prices, payment/delivery terms; de facto e-commerce ontology (now largely merged into schema.org). Stable PURL namespace http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1; canonical RDF/XML file confirmed at heppnetz.de (V1.0, release 2011-10-01). Open, no registration. Wiki: http://wiki.goodrelations-vocabulary.org/.

ontologies.name: ISO 20022 Financial Repository / e-Repository & Data Dictionary
ontologies.license: Free for use (ISO 20022 RA terms; ISO-copyrighted)
ontologies.notes: Universal financial-messaging metamodel: business process catalogue + data dictionary + message definitions (payments, securities, FX, trade). e-Repository delivered in EMF/ecore + XML (requires generating an Eclipse plugin to browse); message schemas (XSD) published per message set under iso20022.org. Homepage/portal entry given because there is no single stable direct-download zip URL — files are version-listed on the site. Free to download under ISO 20022 Registration Authority terms (registration/account may be required for some areas), ISO holds copyright.

ontologies.name: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS 2022)
ontologies.notes: Official US industry classification (hierarchical: sector/subsector/industry group/industry, 2-6 digit). Confirmed direct files: 2022_NAICS_Structure.xlsx, 6-digit_2022_Codes.xlsx, 2022_NAICS_Index_File.xlsx, and the PDF manual, all under census.gov/naics/2022NAICS/. Flat code lists (XLSX/CSV/PDF), not RDF/OWL. US Census Bureau, public domain. Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: O*NET-SOC Occupations Database & Taxonomy
ontologies.notes: US DOL occupational taxonomy + rich occupation attribute data (skills, tasks, work activities). Latest release O*NET 30.3. Confirmed direct downloads: text (tab-delimited) zip above, plus Excel (db_30_3_excel.zip), MySQL/PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle variants at /dl_files/database/. Not RDF/OWL — relational/tab-delimited with data dictionaries. CC-BY-4.0 (credit O*NET + US DOL/ETA). Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: Schema.org vocabulary (incl. commerce/Offer/Product terms)
ontologies.notes: Cross-domain web vocabulary with strong commerce coverage (Offer, Product, PriceSpecification, Organization, Service). Latest release 30.0 (2026-03-25). Full vocab also as TTL/JSON-LD/N-Triples/RDF, e.g. .../data/releases/30.0/schemaorg-all-https.ttl and schemaorg-all-https.jsonld. The .owl is an approximation, not authoritative. Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: US GAAP Financial Reporting Taxonomy (XBRL)
ontologies.license: FASB Taxonomy Terms (free public use; FASB-copyrighted)
ontologies.notes: FASB/SEC US GAAP reporting taxonomy used for SEC filings. Direct zip download confirmed at xbrl.fasb.org (2025 release; 2026 also released by FASB staff). XBRL/XSD+XML schema with linkbases. Free to download, no registration; FASB holds copyright and publishes terms of use. XBRL US (https://xbrl.us/xbrl-taxonomy/2025-us-gaap/) provides a mirrored/packaged copy plus SEC Reporting + DQC rules taxonomies.

ontologies.name: NASA GCMD Keywords (Global Change Master Directory)
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. Hierarchical controlled Earth-science vocabularies (science keywords, platforms, instruments, providers, locations, etc.) served as SKOS Concepts (RDF), OWL, XML, CSV by NASA's Keyword Management System (KMS) REST API. Example URL fetches the sciencekeywords scheme; use /concepts?format=rdf for all, or /concepts/concept_scheme/{scheme}. Mirror host https://cmr.earthdata.nasa.gov/kms/. U.S. federal data — effectively public domain. Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: GeoNames Ontology
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. This is the OWL/RDF schema (classes & properties; feature codes in SKOS) for describing the GeoNames gazetteer as linked data. The ontology schema itself is small. NOTE: the full GeoNames RDF data DUMP (~12M features, ~182M triples, multi-GB) is a separate download from the GeoNames export page and is also CC-BY-4.0. Open, no registration for the ontology.

ontologies.name: CGI/IUGS GeoSciML Geoscience Vocabularies (e.g. Simple Lithology)
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. SKOS/RDF controlled vocabularies (lithology, geologic unit type, commodities, contacts, etc.) maintained by the IUGS CGI Geoscience Terminology Working Group, served from resource.geosciml.org (content-negotiated RDF/XML, Turtle, JSON). Lithology vocab has ~265 rock names. 30+ vocabularies in the register at http://resource.geosciml.org/def/voc/. Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: OGC GeoSPARQL Ontology (v1.1)
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. The core RDF/OWL ontology of the OGC GeoSPARQL standard for representing geographic features and geometries and querying them in SPARQL. Namespace http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#. OGC publishes the ontology/data under Apache-2.0 (documents under separate OGC doc license). Small file; open, no registration.

ontologies.name: ISC/CGI Geologic Timescale Ontology
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. OWL ontology (RDF representation of the ICS International Chronostratigraphic Chart) aligned with OWL-Time, SOSA and GeoSPARQL; maintained by CGI-IUGS. Repository (github.com/CGI-IUGS/timescale-data) is CC0-1.0. Content-negotiated at resource.geosciml.org. Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: INSPIRE Registry (EU code lists & themes)
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. SKOS/RDF representations of the code lists, spatial-data themes and application-schema enumerations defined by the EU INSPIRE Directive (2007/2/EC) and Regulation 1089/2010. The registry.en.rdf entry-point lists the sub-registers; individual code lists are dereferenceable as RDF (append the item URI). Code-list spatial object types align with GeoSPARQL. Open (EU reuse / CC-BY), no registration.

ontologies.name: IVOA Astronomical Object Types Vocabulary (Virtual Observatory / IAU-endorsed semantics)
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. SKOS/RDF vocabulary of astronomical object types (stars, galaxies, etc.) maintained by the IVOA Semantics Working Group; content-negotiated (RDF/XML, Turtle, desise). IVOA vocabularies are CC0. The IVOA RDF index at www.ivoa.net/rdf also hosts UCD1+ (Unified Content Descriptors), reference frames, time scales, messengers, data product types — the astronomy/space controlled vocabularies. Open, no registration. (Note: classic OWL ObjectTypes_0.99r.owl predecessor is superseded by this.)

ontologies.name: SOSA — Sensor, Observation, Sample, and Actuator ontology (W3C/OGC)
ontologies.notes: Verified 200 (W3C namespace serves the ontology via content negotiation; request Turtle). Lightweight core for sensors/observations/samples/actuators; the elementary module of SSN. Joint W3C/OGC Recommendation (2017, 2023 edition). Open, no registration. Source repo: github.com/w3c/sdw-sosa-ssn.

ontologies.name: SSN — Semantic Sensor Network ontology (W3C/OGC)
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. Full SSN ontology that extends/imports SOSA with richer axiomatization (systems, deployments, properties). Namespace http://www.w3.org/ns/ssn/. Stable namespace also at https://www.w3.org/ns/ssn/ (content-negotiated). Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: SWEET — Semantic Web for Earth and Environmental Terminology
ontologies.notes: Verified 200. Modular suite of ~200 ontologies / ~6000 concepts for Earth system science, governed by ESIP. The sweetAll.ttl is the merged ontology in Turtle. Persistent OWL endpoint also at http://sweetontology.net/sweetAll (content-negotiated). License changed to CC0 1.0 at v3.5.0 (Jul 2022); earlier versions were Apache-2.0. Open, no registration.

ontologies.name: NOMEN - A nomenclatural ontology for biological names
Ontology of Biological and Clinical Statistics
Akoma Ntoso (OASIS LegalDocML) XML schema
ontologies.license: OASIS IPR Policy (open standard, free to use)
ontologies.notes: Official OASIS Standard XSD (akomantoso30.xsd, v3.0, ~239 KB) verified HTTP 200; companion xml.xsd in same directory. International technical standard (XML vocabulary) for machine-readable legislative, judiciary and parliamentary documents. Namespace http://docs.oasis-open.org/legaldocml/ns/akn/3.0. Source repo: github.com/oasis-open/legaldocml-akomantoso. It is an XML schema/vocabulary rather than an OWL ontology.

ontologies.name: Common Data Model (CDM) — EU Publications Office / EUR-Lex ontology
ontologies.notes: FRBR-compliant OWL ontology that semantically describes all CELLAR content (EUR-Lex, OP Portal): Official Journal, case-law, consolidated legislation, preparatory acts, etc. Dereferenceable URI verified HTTP 200 returning application/rdf+xml via content negotiation (Accept: application/rdf+xml). It is the actual ontology behind the EU's ELI/ECLI implementations and the public EUR-Lex SPARQL endpoint.

ontologies.name: Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC)
ontologies.license: Public / freely available (EPO-USPTO joint classification; no explicit open license, free to download and reuse)
ontologies.notes: Verified HTTP 200 (2026-06 monthly release; filename embeds YYYYMM e.g. CPCSchemeXML202605.zip, so URL changes monthly). Companion file: FullCPCDefinitionXML202605.zip. XSD schemas (CPCSchemeSchema17.xsd, CPCDefinitionsSchema10.xsd) also on the bulk page. Hierarchical patent classification (sections A-H + Y). Managed by EPO; mirrored at USPTO Open Data (data.uspto.gov/bulkdata) as the CPC Master Classification File. No registration required.

ontologies.name: ELI — European Legislation Identifier ontology
ontologies.notes: Direct eli.owl reference file verified HTTP 200. Common data model (OWL) for exchanging legislation metadata on the web; underpins EUR-Lex. Note: ECLI (European Case Law Identifier) is a sibling identifier scheme, not a separately downloadable ontology — its metadata reuses ELI/CDM RDF. An extension, ELI-DL (draft legislation), is packaged on the Interoperable Europe portal.

ontologies.name: EuroVoc — Multilingual EU Thesaurus
ontologies.license: CC BY 4.0 (EU Publications Office reuse policy)
ontologies.notes: Homepage verified HTTP 200; current release v4.23. Multilingual (24+ EU languages), multidisciplinary thesaurus covering EU activities. Available as SKOS-Core, SKOS-AP-EU (RDF), XML, MARC-XML, Excel. Downloads are served from the CELLAR repository via the EU Vocabularies 'Downloads' tab (JavaScript-rendered, so no single static file URL given here); SPARQL endpoint also available. Reuse under CC BY 4.0 per EU Publications Office policy.

ontologies.name: FRBRoo — FRBR object-oriented (legal/bibliographic FRBR for works)
ontologies.license: Free to use (CIDOC CRM / IFLA; no explicit SPDX license stated)
ontologies.notes: FRBRoo v2.4 RDFS file (~117 KB) verified HTTP 200. Object-oriented FRBR model harmonised with CIDOC CRM, used in legal contexts to model the Work/Expression/Manifestation hierarchy of legislation (e.g. it is imported by ELI-DL and underlies the EU CDM's FRBR layering). Note: FRBRoo has been superseded by LRMoo v1.0 (approved Apr 2024) on the same site; FRBRoo 2.4 remains downloadable. Filename labeled 'draft' but is the published 2.4 RDFS.

ontologies.name: International Patent Classification (IPC)
ontologies.license: WIPO terms of use; master files freely available for download (no open SPDX license; see WIPO terms)
ontologies.notes: Homepage/download-area only (verified HTTP 200) — no single stable direct-download URL; the IPC Download and IT Support Area hosts versioned master files (scheme/compilation XML zipped with DTD, plus TXT/PDF, e.g. IPC-2026.01). Browse https://ipcpub.wipo.int for the online publication. WIPO's master classification of patents, basis for CPC. Free; terms at https://www.wipo.int/en/web/terms-of-use.

ontologies.name: LKIF-core — Legal Knowledge Interchange Format core ontology
ontologies.notes: Direct raw OWL file verified HTTP 200. Modular basic ontology of legal concepts (norms, legal actions/roles, agents, time, processes, mereology) from the EU ESTRELLA project. ~15 interlinked modules; each .owl has a matching .ttl. Raw path pattern: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RinkeHoekstra/lkif-core/master/<file>.owl (e.g. norm.owl, legal-action.owl). LICENSE file added in repo; CC BY 4.0.

ontologies.name: ConceptNet 5.7
ontologies.notes: Multilingual commonsense knowledge graph. Distributed as a gzipped tab-separated edges (assertions) file, NOT native RDF/OWL despite being a graph; format field set to csv accordingly. Latest release is 5.7.0 (2019). Some constituent sources carry additional license terms; see the 'Copying and sharing ConceptNet' page.

ontologies.name: DBpedia Ontology (DBO)
ontologies.notes: Cross-domain schema/T-Box (~768 classes, ~3000 properties) generated from the DBpedia Mappings Wiki. URL is the DBpedia Archivo download endpoint that redirects to the latest OWL; add &f=ttl or &f=nt for other formats, or &dev for the development version. This is the schema only, not the instance data.

ontologies.name: Open English WordNet (2025)
ontologies.notes: Community fork/successor of Princeton WordNet, maintained by the Global WordNet Association. Direct gzipped Turtle (RDF, OntoLex-Lemon model). WN-LMF XML also available at https://en-word.net/static/english-wordnet-2025.xml.gz . Fully open, no registration. Filename embeds the release year; update the year for newer releases.

ontologies.name: OLiA - Ontologies of Linguistic Annotation
ontologies.notes: OWL2/DL reference model + annotation/linking models for tagsets across 85+ languages (ACoLi Lab). Core ontology resolvable via PURL (http://purl.org/olia/olia.owl); individual annotation models e.g. http://purl.org/olia/penn.owl . Full repo at https://github.com/acoli-repo/olia .

ontologies.name: OntoLex-Lemon (Lexicon Model for Ontologies, Core)
ontologies.notes: W3C Community Group de-facto standard vocabulary for publishing lexical data as linked data. Verified raw OWL file (owl:Ontology http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex , v1.1, CC0). Companion modules (lime, vartrans, decomp, synsem) live in the same GitHub repo and under the W3C namespace http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/.

ontologies.name: Princeton WordNet 3.1 (RDF)
ontologies.notes: Canonical Princeton WordNet mapped to RDF using the OntoLex-Lemon model; per-synset/sense dereferenceable URIs and bulk Turtle. URL given is the about/download landing page (the linked bulk .ttl/.nt files are versioned there) rather than a single hard-coded dump path. Permissive WordNet license.

ontologies.name: Universal Dependencies (treebanks)
ontologies.license: Per-treebank (mostly CC BY-SA / CC BY-NC-SA variants)
ontologies.notes: Cross-linguistic morphosyntactic annotation framework + treebanks (v2.16: 319 treebanks, 179 languages, May 2025). Data is CoNLL-U (plain text), NOT OWL/XML markup - 'xml' chosen only because CoNLL-U is not in the format enum; treat as CoNLL-U. URL is the versioned LINDAT/CLARIAH-CZ handle for the v2.16 release. Each treebank has its own license - you must comply per treebank. Released via https://universaldependencies.org/download.html .

ontologies.name: Wikidata (RDF dumps)
ontologies.notes: Collaborative cross-domain knowledge graph. CAUTION: enormous scale - full latest-all.ttl is hundreds of GB compressed; the 'truthy' dump (latest-truthy.nt.bz2, direct statements only) is the smaller practical option. URL is the entities dump directory (dated subfolders + latest-all/latest-truthy files); also has dated NTriples dumps. Free, CC0, no registration.

ontologies.name: YAGO 4.5
ontologies.notes: Large clean knowledge base built on Wikidata taxonomy + schema.org, with a rich SHACL-constrained schema. Released as a set of Turtle files (Schema, Taxonomy, Facts, Facts-beyond-Wikipedia, Meta). URL is the official downloads page (files also mirrored on Zenodo); NT format also offered. Stable release 4.5 (May 2023).

ontologies.name: BattINFO - Battery Interface Ontology (EMMO domain-battery)
ontologies.notes: Repository VERIFIED. EMMO-aligned domain ontology for battery cells, materials, methods and data (EU BIG-MAP project). Raw file: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BIG-MAP/BattINFO/master/battinfo.ttl . Companion EMMO battery-domain docs at https://emmo-repo.github.io/domain-battery/ . Accessible from Python via EMMOntoPy. Strong rare/flagship-domain (energy materials) candidate.

ontologies.name: CHAMEO - Characterisation Methodology Domain Ontology
ontologies.notes: Repository VERIFIED. EMMO-based domain ontology harmonising materials characterisation methods/experiments (enables FAIR characterisation documentation). Direct file e.g. https://raw.githubusercontent.com/emmo-repo/domain-characterisation-methodology/main/chameo.ttl . Published IRI http://emmo.info/emmo/domain/chameo ; w3id namespace https://w3id.org/emmo/domain/characterisation-methodology/chameo# . Also archived on Zenodo (record 8318142).

ontologies.name: EMMO - Elementary Multiperspective Material Ontology (formerly European Materials Modelling Ontology)
ontologies.notes: VERIFIED direct download (raw TTL resolves, not 404). Latest v1.0.3 (Jan 2026), middle-level ontology serialized as emmo.ttl; also emax.ttl (maximal), tlo.ttl (top-level), mlo.ttl (middle-level). Persistent IRI https://w3id.org/emmo/emmo. DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5730500. The flagship foundational/top-level ontology for materials modelling; many domain ontologies (battery, characterisation, crystallography) are built on it. OWL2-DL.

ontologies.name: MAMBO - Materials And Molecules Basic Ontology
ontologies.notes: Repository VERIFIED. Lightweight, modular ontology for multiscale molecular materials (nanomaterials, supramolecular systems, molecular aggregates) bridging molecular and higher-scale domains; core concepts Material/Calculation/Measurement. Listed on MatPortal. CAVEAT: confirm exact SPDX license in the GitHub repo before redistribution.

ontologies.name: MatOnto - Materials Ontology (DOLCE-based)
ontologies.license: See repository (open, GitHub-hosted; license not explicitly stated on landing page)
ontologies.notes: Repository VERIFIED. Extensible materials ontology based on the DOLCE upper ontology; TTL files include matonto.ttl plus domain modules (e.g. ceramic.ttl). Direct file: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/inovexcorp/MatOnto-Ontologies/master/matonto.ttl . CAVEAT: confirm exact license in the repo before redistribution - landing page does not advertise a clear SPDX license; flagged as open-but-verify. Older / less actively maintained than EMMO/PMDco.

ontologies.name: MDO - Materials Design Ontology
ontologies.notes: Repository VERIFIED. Modular OWL2-DL ontology (Core, Structure, Calculation, Provenance modules) for computational/solid-state materials design. Individual modules downloadable as OWL, e.g. https://liusemweb.github.io/mdo/core/1.0/mdo-core.owl ; full at https://liusemweb.github.io/mdo/full/1.1/. w3id PURL reserved. MIT license is unusual-but-valid for an ontology; permits free reuse.

ontologies.name: MSEO - Materials Science and Engineering Ontology (Mat-O-Lab)
ontologies.notes: Repository VERIFIED. Mid- and domain-level MSE ontology built on the IOF (Industrial Ontologies Foundry) / BFO stack; Fraunhofer (IWM, EMI, ISC) contributors. ~150 classes / 890 axioms. Also listed on MatPortal (https://matportal.org/ontologies/MSEO). Good Industry-4.0 / experiment-data representation angle. Confirm license file in repo.

ontologies.name: NPO - NanoParticle Ontology
ontologies.license: CC (Creative Commons; per BioPortal metadata) - verify
ontologies.notes: BFO-framework OWL ontology for nanomaterial preparation, chemical composition and characterisation (cancer nanotech / nanomedicine origin - more nano-bio than pure MSE, but covers nanomaterials). FREE-WITH-REGISTRATION: programmatic/direct OWL download from BioPortal needs a free NCBO BioPortal account + API key (PURL http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/npo). Unauthenticated BioPortal fetch returns 403. A mirror copy exists at aber-owl.net (http://aber-owl.net/ontology/NPO). Flag the registration requirement.

ontologies.name: PeriodicTable.owl - Periodic Table of the Elements in OWL (DAML)
ontologies.license: Public / open reference data (no explicit restrictive license stated)
ontologies.notes: VERIFIED stable direct OWL file. Classic, long-standing reference ontology of the chemical elements (maintained by Mike Dean, orig. Michael Cook DAML+OIL). Lightweight element reference data useful as a building block for materials/chemistry knowledge graphs. Old but stable URL; not actively developed. Alternative modern community option: Ontology of Chemical Elements (OCE), https://github.com/ontologyofchemicalelements/OCE .

ontologies.name: PMDco - Platform MaterialDigital Core Ontology
ontologies.notes: VERIFIED direct download (raw TTL resolves, valid v3.0.0 content). Mid-level MSE ontology built on BFO, reuses RO/IAO/OBI; follows processing-structure-properties paradigm. Namespace https://w3id.org/pmd/co. Also published as .owl and Widoco HTML docs at https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/. German MaterialDigital platform. Note: v3.x not backward-compatible with 2.x.

ontologies.name: QUDT - Quantities, Units, Dimensions and Types Ontology
ontologies.notes: VERIFIED: latest release v3.3.0 (May 2026). No single stable raw all-in-one TTL on a fixed branch path (tested raw path 404s); the stable download is the GitHub release asset qudt-public-repo-3.3.0.zip (~10.8 MB) containing QUDT-all-in-one-OWL.ttl and QUDT-all-in-one-SHACL.ttl. Resolvable graph also at http://qudt.org/qudt-all (SHACL) / http://qudt.org/shacl/qudt-all. Units/quantities backbone reused widely across materials ontologies (e.g. PMDco maps to QUDT). OWL serialized in Turtle.

ontologies.name: VIMMP - Virtual Materials Marketplace Ontologies
ontologies.notes: Repository VERIFIED (GitLab). System of OWL2/Turtle marketplace-level ontologies characterising materials-modelling services, models and user interactions; uses EMMO as top-level ontology. Multiple TTL files in the repo. CAVEAT: confirm exact license in the GitLab LICENSE file before redistribution.

ontologies.name: CTO: Core Ontology of Clinical Trials
Ontology for Modeling and Representation of Social Entities
Ontology of Organizational Structures of Trauma centers and Trauma systems
Gender, Sex, and Sexual Orientation (GSSO) ontology
Ontology of Precision Medicine and Investigation
Scientific Evidence and Provenance Information Ontology
OntoAvida: ontology for Avida digital evolution platform
Core Ontology for Biology and Biomedicine
DOLCE+DnS Ultralite (DUL) / DOLCE foundational ontology
ontologies.license: CC BY (LOA-CNR); see homepage for terms
ontologies.notes: Descriptive Ontology for Linguistic and Cognitive Engineering, maintained by ISTC-CNR Laboratory for Applied Ontology. DUL is the widely-used lightweight OWL version (xml:base loa-cnr.it). Other modules at the same path: DOLCE-Lite.owl. Full DOLCE in CLIF/FOL and OWL plus papers on homepage. Note: legacy loa-cnr.it host URLs sometimes appear in metadata; the loa.istc.cnr.it host is the active one.

ontologies.name: gist — minimalist enterprise upper ontology (Semantic Arts)
ontologies.notes: ~100 classes; pragmatic business upper ontology. Latest release v14.1.0 (April 2026); source Turtle file gistCore.ttl in the 'ontologies' dir of the repo, release assets named e.g. gistCore14.1.0.ttl. No single permanent latest-file PURL — use the GitHub releases page (link above) or downloads.semanticarts.com. Reference data (units of measure, etc.) in separate repo semanticarts/gistReferenceData.

ontologies.name: gUFO — gentle/lightweight Unified Foundational Ontology (UFO)
ontologies.notes: OWL 2 DL implementation of UFO (NEMO/UFES, Almeida, Guizzardi, Sales, Falbo). Ontology IRI http://purl.org/nemo/gufo#; PURL for Protege import https://purl.org/nemo/gufo#. Full UFO itself is a theory (papers/OntoUML), gUFO is the machine-readable lightweight artifact. Docs at http://purl.org/nemo/doc/gufo.

ontologies.name: OM — Ontology of units of Measure 2.0
ontologies.notes: OWL 2 ontology of units, quantities, measures, dimensions (H. Rijgersberg et al., Wageningen). Direct raw RDF/XML download above; base URI http://www.ontology-of-units-of-measure.org/resource/om-2/. UCUM mappings provided in om-2-ucum.ttl in the same repo. Mirror OWL copy on aber-owl.net. Stable and maintained.

ontologies.name: OMG Commons — Quantities and Units ontology
ontologies.license: OMG specification terms (Non-Assert IPR mode); freely downloadable
ontologies.notes: Part of the OMG Commons Ontology Library (used by FIBO etc.). 'Latest' RDF and TTL at https://www.omg.org/spec/Commons/QuantitiesAndUnits.rdf and .../QuantitiesAndUnits.ttl ; dated versions e.g. .../20250801/QuantitiesAndUnits.ttl. Provides core concepts for quantities, units, systems of quantities/units aligned to ISO 80000 ISQ. License is OMG's own terms (informational, Non-Assert), not CC/OSI — flag.

ontologies.name: OpenCyc (open subset of Cyc)
ontologies.notes: DISCONTINUED by Cycorp in 2017 — no longer officially distributed. OpenCyc 4.0 (~239k concepts, OWL) survives only via third-party mirrors (GitHub above, SourceForge https://sourceforge.net/projects/opencyc/) and the LOD cloud. No official/current support; data is from 2012. Full Cyc is commercial/closed. Include only as a historical/archival resource.

ontologies.name: BIPM SI Reference Point (SI Digital Framework knowledge graphs)
ontologies.license: Not explicitly stated in repo README (LICENCE file present; verify before redistribution) — official BIPM resource, publicly available
ontologies.notes: Official BIPM digital, FAIR, machine-actionable encoding of the SI Brochure. Five pillars — units, prefixes, decisions, constants, quantities — each a knowledge graph downloadable as TTL, with public SPARQL endpoint and API at si-digital-framework.org/SI. Repo has SI_Reference_Point/ and knowledge_graphs/ folders. Authoritative metrology source aligned with QUDT. Confirm exact LICENCE terms in repo before reuse.

ontologies.name: Suggested Upper Merged Ontology (SUMO) + MILO mid-level ontology
ontologies.license: GNU GPL (extension ontologies); core SUMO free, owned by IEEE
ontologies.notes: Largest formal public ontology; native format is SUO-KIF (.kif files in the GitHub repo, including Merge.kif and Mid-level-ontology.kif/MILO). The authoritative form is KIF, not OWL. A provisional, lossy OWL translation is downloadable at http://www.adampease.org/OP/SUMO.owl. No single stable OWL release asset; clone the repo for full KIF source. MILO copyrighted Teknowledge/Articulate Software, GPL.

ontologies.name: UCUM — Unified Code for Units of Measure
ontologies.license: UCUM v1.0 License (Regenstrief Institute) — free, royalty-free, but custom (not OSI/CC); attribution + no-modification of the code system
ontologies.notes: Code system (not an OWL ontology) of units for science/engineering/medicine; canonical machine-readable artifact is ucum-essence.xml. Source repo https://github.com/ucum-org/ucum (also https://ucum.org/ucum-essence.xml). License is permissive/free but a bespoke Regenstrief license (https://ucum.org/license), NOT open-source/CC — flag for redistribution. Widely used in HL7/FHIR; an HL7 THO XML CodeSystem representation also exists.

ontologies.name: CIDOC Conceptual Reference Model (CIDOC-CRM)
ontologies.license: Free to use (CIDOC-CRM SIG; ISO 21127). Open access
ontologies.notes: ISO 21127 reference ontology for cultural-heritage/museum information. RDFS encoding of latest official version (7.1.1). Content-negotiated RDF/XML also resolves from https://cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/. An alternative OWL-DL implementation exists as Erlangen CRM (erlangen-crm.org).

ontologies.name: DCAT (Data Catalog Vocabulary)
ontologies.notes: W3C Recommendation, used for describing data catalogs/datasets on the web (open-data portals). Namespace http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#. Content negotiation from https://www.w3.org/ns/dcat serves Turtle/RDF-XML/JSON-LD. DCAT3 serializations also at github.com/w3c/dxwg (dcat/rdf/dcat3.ttl).

ontologies.name: DCMI Metadata Terms (Dublin Core / DCTERMS)
ontologies.notes: Core metadata vocabulary. Two namespaces: legacy elements http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/ and full terms http://purl.org/dc/terms/. DCMI publishes RDF/XML and Turtle representations; a stable dated snapshot also exists, e.g. http://dublincore.org/2020/01/20/dublin_core_terms.ttl (.rdf available too). Verify current filename on the dcmi-terms page.

ontologies.name: Europeana Data Model (EDM)
ontologies.notes: Cultural-heritage aggregation model used by Europeana, built on RDF(S), OAI-ORE, SKOS, Dublin Core. Direct OWL at the listed URL; namespace http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/. Also reachable by content negotiation.

ontologies.name: FOAF (Friend of a Friend)
ontologies.notes: Social/people vocabulary (persons, accounts, relationships). Latest spec v0.99 (Jan 2014). Namespace http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/. RDF/XML available by direct link and by content negotiation from the namespace URI.

ontologies.name: FRBR (Functional Requirements for Bibliographic Records) — core RDF
ontologies.license: CC-BY (SPAR FRBR); vocab.org core is open
ontologies.notes: Two common RDF expressions: (1) Ian Davis 'core' vocab at http://vocab.org/frbr/core (namespace http://purl.org/vocab/frbr/core#); (2) SPAR 'Essential FRBR in OWL2 DL' at the listed URL http://purl.org/spar/frbr (content-negotiated RDF/XML, Turtle, N-Triples, JSON-LD; source github.com/SPAROntologies/frbr). IFLA also maintains FRBRer at the IFLA/Open Metadata Registry.

ontologies.name: Getty Art & Architecture Thesaurus (AAT) — LOD
ontologies.notes: Thesaurus of art/architecture terms (~250k terms), published as SKOS LOD at vocab.getty.edu. Full N-Triples dumps refreshed monthly; per-record available in JSON/RDF/Turtle/N-Triples; SPARQL at http://vocab.getty.edu/sparql. Confirm exact dump filename via the Getty download page (download.html) and vocab.getty.edu/doc — the full.zip path may vary by release. ULAN and TGN companion vocabularies released the same way.

ontologies.name: Library of Congress Subject Headings (LCSH) — SKOS
ontologies.license: No known restrictions / public (US Library of Congress)
ontologies.notes: Full LCSH as SKOS/RDF. Bulk gzip dumps in N-Triples (~101MB), Turtle (subjects.skosrdf.ttl.gz, ~40MB), RDF/XML (subjects.skosrdf.xml.gz), JSON-LD (subjects.skosrdf.jsonld.gz) from id.loc.gov/download/. Per-resource serializations via content negotiation.

ontologies.name: MeSH (Medical Subject Headings) — NLM RDF
ontologies.license: Free; NLM terms of use (no UMLS license required for MeSH itself)
ontologies.notes: NLM controlled vocabulary (biomedical/library indexing), production RDF since 2017. Bulk N-Triples plus the XML source and XSLT available from NLM's RDF download area (historically ftp://ftp.nlm.nih.gov/online/mesh/rdf; now served via nlmpubs.nlm.nih.gov). SPARQL/LOD at id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh. MeSH itself is freely downloadable; only the UMLS Metathesaurus (which bundles MeSH with other vocabularies) requires a UMLS license. 2026 datasets released. Confirm current path on the meshb/id.nlm download page.

ontologies.name: W3C Organization Ontology (ORG)
ontologies.notes: W3C Recommendation for organizational structures (organizations, roles, memberships, sites) — relevant to social/institutional and library/government linked data. Namespace http://www.w3.org/ns/org#. Turtle and RDF/XML served by content negotiation from https://www.w3.org/ns/org.

ontologies.name: PROV-O (The PROV Ontology)
ontologies.notes: W3C Recommendation (Apr 2013). OWL2 ontology for provenance (entities, activities, agents). Namespace http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#. Direct OWL/Turtle at https://www.w3.org/ns/prov-o.owl and https://www.w3.org/ns/prov-o (content negotiation).

ontologies.name: SKOS (Simple Knowledge Organization System)
ontologies.notes: W3C Recommendation (Aug 2009). The core RDF/OWL vocabulary for thesauri/taxonomies. Namespace http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#. Direct RDF/XML at the listed URL; also served by content negotiation from the namespace. Used as the encoding for AAT, LCSH, MeSH, etc.

ontologies.name: VIAF (Virtual International Authority File)
ontologies.notes: OCLC name-authority aggregation across national libraries (persons, corporate bodies, works), expressed with SKOS/FOAF/RDA/Schema.org. HOMEPAGE/DATA PAGE ONLY — bulk data dumps (RDF/XML and other formats) are linked from this data page but there is no single stable direct file URL; dumps were last refreshed Aug 2024 and OCLC paused updates pending infrastructure work. Per-record RDF via content negotiation from viaf.org/viaf/{id}/.
